analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 9 finding(s) from 10 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: CLINT permission aliases into PLIC permission in connectivity_mapping: target j==6 is granted when access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i] is set. In the SoC enum, j==6 is PLIC and j==7 is CLINT.
findings.vulnerability_category: route/read/write AXI transactions to target manager/peripheral ports / PLIC target access policy, with SoC enum PLIC=6 and CLINT=7 / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI requesters on slave[0] Ariane core and slave[1] debug-module AXI master entering axi_node_intf_wrap Operation: route/read/write AXI transactions to target manager/peripheral ports Object: PLIC target access policy, with SoC enum PLIC=6 and CLINT=7 Expected guard: connectivity_map_o[i][j] should be mediated by access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] for each requester/target/privilege, unless a safe documented alias exists Observed behavior: connectivity_map_o[i][j] is assigned access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] || ((j==6) && access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]), so CLINT permission grants PLIC connectivity for the same requester and privilege Path: access_ctrl_reg -> access_ctrl[i][j] -> axi_node_intf_wrap.access_ctrl_i -> connectivity_mapping.connectivity_map_o -> axi_node cfg_connectivity_map_i Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester authorized for CLINT may access PLIC even if its PLIC access-control bit is not set, allowing unintended interrupt-controller MMIO access

findings.summary: The wrapper forwards slave AW/AR channels into axi_node, passes start/end address arrays and connectivity map into axi_node, and assigns s_valid_rule='1. Range comparisons and invalid/out-of-range response behavior are not visible in the provided axi_node_intf_wrap facts.
findings.vulnerability_category: route AW/AR transactions by address / Debug, ROM, ROM2, PKT, CLINT, PLIC, UART, AES, SPI, Ethernet, GPIO, and DRAM address regions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI requesters on wrapper slave ports Operation: route AW/AR transactions by address Object: Debug, ROM, ROM2, PKT, CLINT, PLIC, UART, AES, SPI, Ethernet, GPIO, and DRAM address regions Expected guard: AW/AR addresses must match configured start_addr_i/end_addr_i ranges and the connectivity/access-control map before endpoint valid can reach a target Observed behavior: The wrapper forwards slave AW/AR channels into axi_node, passes start/end address arrays and connectivity map into axi_node, and assigns s_valid_rule='1. Range comparisons and invalid/out-of-range response behavior are not visible in the provided axi_node_intf_wrap facts. Path: slave[j].aw/ar_* -> s_slave_aw/ar_* -> axi_node_i -> s_master_aw/ar_* -> master[i].aw/ar_* Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If axi_node treats all rules as valid without enforcing start/end bounds, address-based isolation could be broadened. If axi_node enforces cfg_START/END normally, the obligation may be satisfied.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Internal axi_node range comparison, rule-valid semantics, and invalid access response behavior are not included.

findings.summary: Visible debug request path is gated by source selection jtag_enable[0], not by an explicit authorization/key/lifecycle check before dm_top accepts DMI requests.
findings.vulnerability_category: issue DMI read/write/debug operations and route debug-module AXI master accesses / dm_top debug module, Debug region, and AXI-reachable memory/MMIO through dm_axi_m_req / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/SimDTM/DMI debug requester Operation: issue DMI read/write/debug operations and route debug-module AXI master accesses Object: dm_top debug module, Debug region, and AXI-reachable memory/MMIO through dm_axi_m_req Expected guard: Debug/DMI requests must require debug enable plus authentication/key/lifecycle authorization before reaching dm_top and protected assets Observed behavior: debug_req_valid, debug_resp_ready, and debug_req are muxed between JTAG and SimDTM paths based only on jtag_enable[0]. jtag_key is wired to dmi_jtag and ariane_peripherals, but no visible authorization gate blocks debug_req_valid or debug_req before dm_top. Path: SimJTAG/dmi_jtag or SimDTM -> debug_req_valid/debug_req mux -> dm_top DMI interface -> dm_axi_m_req -> axi_master_connect -> slave[1] requester Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Unauthenticated debug transactions may control debug state or access/modify protected assets through the debug module's AXI master path

findings.summary: dm_top outputs ndmreset and debug_req_core directly influence reset/core debug request, with no visible authorization gate between DMI/JTAG requests and these controls.
findings.vulnerability_category: trigger non-debug-module reset and core debug/halt request / system/core reset through ndmreset/ndmreset_n and core debug_req_i through debug_req_core / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/DMI debug interface through dm_top Operation: trigger non-debug-module reset and core debug/halt request Object: system/core reset through ndmreset/ndmreset_n and core debug_req_i through debug_req_core Expected guard: Debug-triggered reset/halt/control signals must require authorized debug state and lifecycle permission Observed behavior: dm_top drives ndmreset_o and debug_req_o. ndmreset is combined into rstgen reset input as rst_ni & (~ndmreset); debug_req_core connects directly to Ariane debug_req_i. Visible harness does not gate these with authentication. Path: DMI/JTAG request mux -> dm_top -> ndmreset_o/debug_req_o -> rstgen and Ariane core Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A debug requester may reset or halt/debug the core without a visible authorization check, disrupting protected state or privilege flow
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dm_top internal authentication/authorization behavior is not shown.

findings.summary: rom2 secure_reg, described as holding keys/access-control values, has a req_i && we_i write path with no visible lock, privilege, update-mode, or bounds guard in the rom2 module.
findings.vulnerability_category: write secure_reg and read rdata_o / rom2 secure_reg array initialized from mem containing AES/JTAG/access-control values / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: MMIO/AXI requester reaching the ROM2/peripheral path Operation: write secure_reg and read rdata_o Object: rom2 secure_reg array initialized from mem containing AES/JTAG/access-control values Expected guard: ROM2 contents should be immutable or writable only in a privileged loader/update state; read access to protected contents should be mediated by bus policy Observed behavior: On reset secure_reg <= mem. During normal operation, if req_i is asserted and we_i is high, secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i. There is no local privilege, lock, or update-mode condition. Path: ROM2Base address region/master[ariane_soc::ROM2] -> rom2_fuse/peripheral wiring -> rom2 req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i -> secure_reg -> rdata_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester with ROM2 write reachability can alter key material or access-control configuration values, undermining secret integrity and permission policy
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The exact ariane_peripherals-to-rom2 wiring is not fully shown, but the module-local write path is unguarded.

findings.summary: ROM2 read path has an in-range guard in rdata_o, but the write path directly indexes secure_reg with addr_i-derived bits and has no explicit index < RomSize guard.
findings.vulnerability_category: address-indexed write/read to secure_reg / rom2 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Requester driving rom2 req_i/we_i/addr_i Operation: address-indexed write/read to secure_reg Object: rom2 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] Expected guard: Both read and write state updates must enforce index < RomSize; malformed/out-of-range addresses must not corrupt protected state Observed behavior: raddr_q is assigned the extracted address index on reads, and rdata_o checks raddr_q < RomSize. On writes, secure_reg[extracted_index] <= wdata_i occurs without an explicit bounds condition. Path: addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3] -> raddr_q/read or secure_reg write index Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Depending on parameterization and synthesis/simulation semantics, unguarded writes could alias or corrupt protected storage; even with current RomSize=4, write protection relies on implicit truncation rather than a policy guard

findings.summary: access_ctrl_reg is sourced through ariane_peripherals/ROM2 fuse path, and ROM2 locally exposes a write path to values identified as access-control words. No visible privilege/lock guard protects access_ctrl_reg updates in the provided scope.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure/change access policy / access_ctrl_reg bits sliced into access_ctrl[i][j] and used by connectivity_mapping / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software-visible peripheral path, CPU, or debug master capable of reaching ROM2/peripherals Operation: configure/change access policy Object: access_ctrl_reg bits sliced into access_ctrl[i][j] and used by connectivity_mapping Expected guard: Access-control registers must be writable only by authorized machine/debug subjects and preferably locked; updates must not transiently open protected routes Observed behavior: access_ctrl_reg is declared in the harness, connected to ariane_peripherals, and sliced into access_ctrl[i][j]. The provided facts do not show access_ctrl_reg reset/write decode guards. ROM2 mem comments identify access-control words and rom2 has an unguarded req_i&&we_i write path to secure_reg. Path: ROM2/peripheral access_ctrl_reg source -> access_ctrl[i][j] -> axi_node_intf_wrap.access_ctrl_i -> connectivity_map_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If an unprivileged CPU path or debug master can modify access_ctrl_reg or its ROM2 source, it can open otherwise protected interconnect routes
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Declaration/reset/write logic for access_ctrl_reg inside ariane_peripherals is not visible.

findings.summary: A single global priv_lvl from the Ariane core is used for interconnect access-control lookup for all slave/requester ports, including the debug module AXI master on slave[1].
findings.vulnerability_category: authorize AXI route using privilege-indexed access-control bits / access_ctrl_i requester/target entry indexed by priv_lvl_i / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: CPU and debug-module AXI master requesters Operation: authorize AXI route using privilege-indexed access-control bits Object: access_ctrl_i requester/target entry indexed by priv_lvl_i Expected guard: The privilege/security attribute used for authorization must correspond to the requester and transaction, and must not be globally stale or shared across unrelated masters Observed behavior: priv_lvl is assigned from priv_lvl_processor, which is an Ariane core output. That single priv_lvl is connected to axi_node_intf_wrap. The debug module AXI master is connected as slave[1], but no separate privilege representation for debug/DMA/requester identity is visible. Path: ariane priv_lvl_o -> priv_lvl_processor -> priv_lvl -> axi_node_intf_wrap.priv_lvl_i -> connectivity_mapping access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Debug or other non-core master requests may be authorized using the CPU's current/stale privilege level, causing over-permissive or misattributed access decisions

findings.summary: axi_node_intf_wrap forwards slave aw_prot/ar_prot/user fields into internal signals and forwards master-side aw_prot/ar_prot/user outputs. DRAM bridge maps prot fields through channel converter outputs but assigns dram.aw_user/ar_user/w_user directly from master[DRAM].*_user rather than channel-converter outputs. Facts do not show whether the converter transforms user fields or whether downstream targets rely on user/prot.
findings.vulnerability_category: forward read/write transactions through axi_node_intf_wrap and DRAM bridge / AXI aw_prot/ar_prot and aw_user/ar_user/w_user attributes / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI masters whose prot/user attributes may be consumed downstream Operation: forward read/write transactions through axi_node_intf_wrap and DRAM bridge Object: AXI aw_prot/ar_prot and aw_user/ar_user/w_user attributes Expected guard: Security/privilege attributes must be preserved end-to-end or intentionally recomputed at bridges before downstream permission checks Observed behavior: axi_node_intf_wrap forwards slave aw_prot/ar_prot/user fields into internal signals and forwards master-side aw_prot/ar_prot/user outputs. DRAM bridge maps prot fields through channel converter outputs but assigns dram.aw_user/ar_user/w_user directly from master[DRAM].*_user rather than channel-converter outputs. Facts do not show whether the converter transforms user fields or whether downstream targets rely on user/prot. Path: slave aw/ar/w prot/user -> axi_node_intf_wrap -> master[DRAM] -> channel converter/DRAM assignments Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If downstream permission logic relies on user/prot and bridge paths mismatch converted fields with direct user fields, requests could be misattributed. If downstream does not use these attributes or converter preserves them, the obligation may be satisfied.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Downstream use of prot/user and channel converter user-field behavior are not shown.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
